title: Design data layout for cache locality
description: Lay out hot data so accesses are sequential and dense, maximizing cache-line and prefetcher utilization before touching SIMD or threads.
body: Use when a hot loop is memory-bound and profiling shows high cache-miss rates. Do not jump to SIMD or threading before the access pattern is linear and dense. Actions: convert array-of-structs to struct-of-arrays when only some fields are hot, pad/align hot structures to cache-line boundaries, and inspect the stride so the hardware prefetcher can track it; verify with perf the L1/LLC miss rate drops. Validation signals include a measured reduction in cache misses and improved IPC on representative input, not just a faster microbenchmark. Risk boundary: layout changes ripple through every reader/writer; if the data is shared across modules, escalate and benchmark the whole workload before committing.
